description:
Context:
    You are an automation-workflow specialist and have been asked to add a very small “security sanity check” step to an existing pipeline.  
    The pipeline already places its build artefacts under

        /home/user/workspace/

    Before the pipeline continues, it must create a short report that lists every **world-writable** file (i.e. any file for which the POSIX “other” class has the write bit set) that exists anywhere underneath that directory tree.

    Goal:
    1. Using **exactly one single terminal command**, create the file

           /home/user/workspace/world_writable_report.log

    2. The command must scan **recursively** from /home/user/workspace and detect every regular file whose *other* permission includes write.
    3. The command must write one line per detected file to the log file, with each line following **precisely** this format (no extra spaces):

           <absolute_path>|<octal_permissions>|<symbolic_permissions>

       Examples of the exact format:
           /home/user/workspace/public.log|666|-rw-rw-rw-
           /home/user/workspace/temp/world.sh|777|-rwxrwxrwx
    4. Sorting: the lines in the logfile **must be in standard ASCII lexicographical order by their absolute path**.
    5. If (theoretically) no world-writable files are found, the command must still create the logfile but leave it empty.
    6. Do **not** print anything else to stdout/stderr that will enter the file; the only permanent artefact must be the correctly formatted logfile.

    Constraints & tips:
      • You do NOT have root.  
      • Only one single shell command is allowed (compose it however you like with pipes, subshells, etc.).  
      • The directory /home/user/workspace already exists and contains several files with a variety of permissions.  
      • The automated test will fail if:  
          – the logfile path or name is wrong,  
          – the line format deviates (different delimiter, extra spaces, etc.),  
          – the file list is not alphabetically sorted,  
          – files that are not world-writable appear, or world-writable files are missing.

    Deliverable:
      The file /home/user/workspace/world_writable_report.log in the exact format described above.
truth:
# Initial on-disk setup before the agent starts
    Pre-existing directories & files (relative to /home/user):

      /workspace/                 (directory, 0755)
      /workspace/confidential.txt (regular file, 0600, 1-line content: "TOP SECRET")
      /workspace/public.log       (regular file, 0666, 1-line content: "public info")
      /workspace/script.sh        (regular file, 0755, 1-line content: "#!/bin/sh")
      /workspace/temp/            (directory, 0755)
      /workspace/temp/temp.db     (regular file, 0664, empty)
      /workspace/temp/world.sh    (regular file, 0777, 1-line content: "#!/bin/sh")

    # Expected artefact after correct task completion
    File: /home/user/workspace/world_writable_report.log
    Permissions of the file itself: 0644 (any default is fine; test will only read it)

    Exact expected content (including trailing newline character):

    /home/user/workspace/public.log|666|-rw-rw-rw-
    /home/user/workspace/temp/world.sh|777|-rwxrwxrwx

    Each line ends with '\n'. No extra blank lines before, between, or after these two lines.
name: task_000000_cfedd016
